skill_revision_note: After adding explicit no-risk-matrix / no-verdict-ladder / no-scoring-table rules to SKILL.md and SKILL.zh_CN.md.

scores.notes: Manual trigger; exactly two sections; no risk matrix, tail section, takeoff/landing drift, or redesign.
English response; focused on thin roadmap evidence and blind spots; no roadmap rewrite.
Low-risk translation case stayed concise and did not invent dramatic risks.
Did not continue takeoff; audited the high-level direction only.
Did not turn bold evaluation-system direction into landing plan.
Did not become full code review; focused on cache-fix uncertainty and blind spots.
English enterprise-sales case stayed in two-section crash-audit form.
Simple UTC date case did not catastrophize; stayed concise.
High-impact production database migration uncertainty was explicit without drifting into migration plan.
No explicit trigger case did not run crash-audit automatically.

skill_quality.notes: Independent evaluator found matching English/Chinese skill files, explicit manual-trigger metadata, disciplined two-question scope, concrete workflow/output formats, concise documentation, saved regression scenarios, aligned OpenAI UI metadata, and cross-platform install-simulation coverage.

install_and_windows_compatibility.notes: The deterministic install simulation copies skills/hello-scholar/crash-audit into a temporary Codex home with pathlib/tempfile/shutil.copytree, then verifies SKILL.md, SKILL.zh_CN.md, agents/openai.yaml, frontmatter, triggers, brainstorming boundary, and UI metadata. The reusable test avoids POSIX-only cp/mv/rmdir assumptions.

routing_regression_after_brainstorming_b.reason: Added Do Not Use / 不要使用 rule: route requirements exploration, approach comparison, design/spec work, or design approval flow to brainstorming instead of crash-audit.

scores.notes: When the user explicitly says not to brainstorm or write a spec and asks '坠机一下', crash-audit remains the correct route.
When the user asks to brainstorm a formal skill design spec, crash-audit does not trigger and brainstorming is the correct route.

verification_limits: Scores are subagent rubric evaluations, not deterministic executable tests.
Official quick_validate.py was not used earlier because this environment lacked PyYAML/pip/venv; static frontmatter/content checks were used instead.
